title: Fragmentation Scaling Law
canonical_statement: Recursive fragmentation emerges whenever scalable stitching capacity degrades relative to bounded local closure capacity across recursive participation scale.
deep_compression_statement: Local survivability is not sufficient for recursive viability.

formal_statement.statement: Recursive fragmentation emerges whenever scalable continuity integration collapses relative to preservation of bounded local coherent recurrence across recursive participation scale.
formal_statement.core_distinction: Local closure preserves bounded recurrence, while stitching preserves scalable continuity-transfer integration.
formal_statement.collapse_insight: Collapse begins when scalable stitching fails relative to bounded local closure preservation.

fragmentation_ratio.interpretation: \Phi_{\mathrm{frag}}(n) measures scalable recursive continuity integration normalized by bounded local coherent recurrence preservation.

fragmentation_onset_condition.interpretation: Fragmentation begins whenever scalable continuity integration degrades faster than bounded local coherent recurrence across recursive scale.
fragmentation_onset_condition.deep_form: Relative integration survivability declines across scale.

critical_fragmentation_condition.interpretation: Critical fragmentation emerges when scalable continuity-transfer survivability collapses relative to bounded local continuity preservation.

protected_core_condition.interpretation: Protected core regimes emerge when bounded local continuity stabilization persists while recursively scalable continuity transfer becomes exhausted.
protected_core_condition.deep_form: Local recurrence survives while scalable integration vanishes.

local_global_survivability_relation.interpretation: Locally survivable continuity domains may remain preserved while recursively scalable continuity integration progressively degenerates.

integration_limited_recursive_viability.meaning: Recursive viability becomes integration-limited while local closure remains partially preserved.

recursive_fragmentation_criticality.meaning: The system approaches critical fragmentation through relative stitching collapse.

isolated_protected_core_persistence.meaning: Local continuity cores persist while scalable continuity transfer is exhausted.

fragmentation_acceleration_extension.interpretation: Accelerating fragmentation occurs when relative continuity integration collapses at an increasing rate across recursive scale.

collapse_geometry.statement: Collapse begins when scalable stitching fails relative to bounded local closure preservation.
collapse_geometry.interpretation: Recursive collapse may begin through scalable continuity-transfer failure before bounded local recurrence fails.
collapse_geometry.deep_form: Fragmentation is relative scaling collapse of continuity integration.

recovery_geometry.statement: Recovery requires restoration of scalable stitching relative to local closure preservation.
recovery_geometry.interpretation: Recovery restores recursive viability by increasing scalable continuity-transfer integration relative to bounded local recurrence.

canonical_compression: Recursive fragmentation emerges whenever scalable stitching capacity degrades relative to bounded local closure capacity.
Collapse begins when scalable stitching fails relative to bounded local closure preservation.
Local survivability is not sufficient for recursive viability.
Fragmentation is relative scaling collapse of continuity integration.
deep_compression_points: Closure measures bounded local recurrent survivability.
Fragmentation ratio measures relative integration survivability.
Local recurrence can persist while scalable integration collapses.
Protected cores emerge when stitching vanishes relative to closure.
Critical fragmentation occurs when the fragmentation ratio approaches threshold.
Recovery requires restoring scalable stitching relative to local closure.
one_sentence_lock: Recursive fragmentation occurs when scalable continuity-transfer stitching collapses relative to bounded local closure, preserving local survivability while destroying recursive viability.
